title: Ethereal Reverie II
location: Juruo Golden Beach Gallery · Quanzhou · China
theme:
Ethereal Reverie II marks my second solo exhibition, set along the breathtaking Golden Coast of Quanzhou. The venue itself feels almost like an extension of the exhibition—a pink, castle-like structure perched by the sea. Through its expansive floor-to-ceiling windows, waves can be seen crashing gently against the building, creating a setting that is both intimate and romantic.

The building’s soft palette and cinematic atmosphere inevitably call to mind the visual language of Wes Anderson. I was instantly drawn to it at first sight; its dreamlike quality resonates deeply with the spirit of Ethereal Reverie.

This exhibition presents a total of sixteen works, including Mother of Universe, alongside several additional realist oil paintings that were not part of the original Ethereal Reverie series. Together, they form a dialogue between the imagined and the observed, expanding the narrative of the series into a broader emotional and visual landscape.

title: Ethereal Reverie
theme:
Ethereal Reverie marks a pivotal moment in my artistic evolution. As my practice progressed, I realized that creating in isolation has its limits; art only truly breathes through dialogue. To move beyond the boundaries of the studio, I decided to independently organize this exhibition as a platform for exchange—a space to present over 30 works and engage in constructive critique with both the public and industry professionals.
The title, Ethereal Reverie, reflects my ongoing fascination with the nature of matter: the tension between what we perceive as tangible reality and what we regard as fantasy. The exhibition is structured into three distinct yet interconnected sections:

Section I: Quantum Refraction – Explorations of texture and form inspired by the world-building of science fiction.

Section II: Visions Enchanted – Mixed-media works rooted in ancient mythology, reinterpreted through a contemporary lens.

Section III: Physical Plane– Traditional impressionist pieces that return to the essence of our immediate reality.
